attacking_style: Favors decisive long-range solo attacks in final kilometers rather than short explosive moves. Demonstrates superior pacing judgment when attacking from breakaways or reduced selections, particularly on climbs and punchy finales.
team_tactics: Operates both as protected rider and tactical blocker. Used as positioning/blocking support in dual-rider breakaway tactics (Tour 2018), allowing teammates to conserve energy while maintaining breakaway integrity.
terrain_preference: Versatile across cobbled semi-classics (Omloop) and hilly Ardennes races (Amstel). Shows atypical climbing strength for classics specialist. Thrives on varied terrain requiring tactical positioning over pure power.
vulnerability: Limited observations of weaknesses, but role flexibility suggests less protected status in stage races. May lack pure sprint speed when reduced groups reach organized finales.
key_patterns: Long-range solo attacks with kilometers remaining, not final sprint bursts (Omloop, Dauphine, Amstel).
Superior late-race positioning and pacing judgment over raw power (Omloop chase, Plagne breakaway).
Dual capability: cobbled classics AND hilly Ardennes races demonstrated in same season (2018).
Willingly sacrifices personal result for team tactics, blocking and positioning for teammates (Tour 2018).
Attacks from already-reduced selections or breakaways rather than initiating splits from peloton (Dauphine, Omloop).
form_trajectory: unknown: Large data gap between 2018 wins and sparse 2020-21 observations. 2021 Dauphine breakaway success suggests maintained tactical acumen but insufficient recent evidence for trajectory assessment.
